snippets.title: Construct a masked-token pseudo-label (conceptual)
snippets.code:
sentence = "The cat sat on the mat"
tokens = sentence.split()
mask_idx = 2  # hide "sat"

input_tokens = tokens.copy()
input_tokens[mask_idx] = "[MASK]"
pseudo_label = tokens[mask_idx]

print("input:", " ".join(input_tokens))
print("pseudo-label:", pseudo_label)
snippets.explanation: The target token already exists in the raw text — that is the self-supervised label. BERT tokenizers use subword [MASK] tokens instead of words, but the idea is identical.

snippets.title: Next-token pairs from plain text
snippets.code:
text = "learn with data"
tokens = text.split()

pairs = []
for i in range(len(tokens) - 1):
    context = tokens[: i + 1]
    next_token = tokens[i + 1]
    pairs.append((" ".join(context), next_token))

for ctx, nxt in pairs:
    print(f"context={ctx!r} -> label={nxt!r}")
snippets.explanation: Each context string is the input; the following token from the same document is the pseudo-label. GPT training repeats this at every position in a long corpus.

snippets.title: Rotation pretext labels for an image batch
snippets.code:
import random

# angles in degrees -> class index
ROTATIONS = [0, 90, 180, 270]

batch_size = 4
labels = [random.choice(range(len(ROTATIONS))) for _ in range(batch_size)]
angles = [ROTATIONS[y] for y in labels]

print("pseudo-labels (class ids):", labels)
print("applied rotations:", angles)
snippets.explanation: When you rotate an image programmatically, you know the angle — that becomes the classification target for rotation prediction SSL.

snippets.title: Hugging Face masked LM loss (sketch)
snippets.code:
from transformers import AutoModelForMaskedLM, AutoTokenizer
import torch

tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained("bert-base-uncased")
model = AutoModelForMaskedLM.from_pretrained("bert-base-uncased")

inputs = tokenizer("The cat [MASK] on the mat.", return_tensors="pt")
labels = inputs["input_ids"].clone()
labels[labels != tokenizer.mask_token_id] = -100  # ignore non-masked

outputs = model(**inputs, labels=labels)
print("MLM loss:", outputs.loss.item())
snippets.explanation: Frameworks set labels to -100 on non-masked positions so loss is computed only on pseudo-label tokens — standard self-supervised MLM training.
